data.title: Intel's Supply Challenges Continue — Market Talk
data.description: Intel's first-quarter revenue would have been meaningfully higher if not for continued supply challenges, Chief Financial Officer David Zinsner says during a call with analysts. Demand continues to outpace supply across Intel's businesses, with a particularly big gap for its Xeon server CPUs, the company says. When asked how much revenue Intel continues to miss out on in the second quarter as a result of the supply shortages, Zinsner says the figure is meaningful. "I probably [would] not want to put a specific number. Let's just say it starts with a B," he says.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Australia's PLS Group reports 86% rise in third-quarter spodumene production
data.description:
Australia's PLS Group said on Friday its third-quarter spodumene concentrate production rose 86% from a year ago, backed by strong operations in its Pilgangoora facility and consistently high lithium recovery.

The pure-play lithium miner, formerly Pilbara Minerals, produced 232,436 dry metric tonnes (dmt) of spodumene concentrate, used to make lithium, in the three months ended March 31, higher than the 124,978 dmt recorded in the same period last year, beating the Visible Alpha consensus estimate of 215,000 dmt.
